articles.title: Congress wins two seats, CPI(M) one in Bengal polls, but vote share falls
articles.description: The Congress and CPI(M) saw a decline in vote share despite together winning three seats in West Bengal's assembly elections.

articles.title: Congress weighs CM choice after UDF’s landslide win in Kerala
articles.description: Kerala witnesses a significant political shift as the Congress-led UDF returns to power after a decade. Discussions are underway to select the new Chief Minister from prominent Congress leaders. The incumbent LDF faces a substantial defeat, prompting introspection. The BJP also secures three seats, marking a breakthrough. Voters have expressed their desire for change.

articles.title: ELECTION SHOCKER: No seats for TMC in key regions including Alipurduar and Purulia
articles.description: The West Bengal election results have shown a major setback for the TMC, with the party failing to win any seats in nine districts including Alipurduar, Kalimpong, Purulia, Bankura, Jhargram, and Jalpaiguri. In several regions, despite multiple seats being available, TMC was completely wiped out and could not open its account. The results mark a significant political shift after 15 years of TMC rule in the state. BJP has celebrated a strong performance, with widespread celebrations reported across several cities in India. BJP leader Suvendu Adhikari made sharp remarks after the results, claiming that TMC lacks ideology and would not survive long after the defeat. He also predicted the party’s collapse within 24 hours and criticized its governance and structure. Overall, the outcome reflects a dramatic political change in West Bengal with BJP gaining major momentum and TMC facing its strongest electoral setback.

articles.description: According to the KC group, if the chief minister is to be decided purely on the basis of number strength, Venugopal enjoys a clear majority.

articles.description: BJP’s sweeping victory in West Bengal has triggered a leadership race for the next Chief Minister, with Suvendu Adhikari, Samik Bhattacharya and Agnimitra Paul emerging as frontrunners.

articles.title: West Bengal Verdict: Three Key BJP Candidates Win Across Hingalganj, Panihati and Ausgram
articles.description: A strong political wave is seen in West Bengal as three prominent BJP-linked candidates register major victories across key constituencies. Rekha Patra, who became a prominent face during the Sandeshkhali protests, has won from Hingalganj by 5,421 votes. Ratna Debnath, mother of the RG Kar hospital victim, has secured a decisive win in Panihati by 28,836 votes. Meanwhile, Kalita Maji, a grassroots BJP worker and house help from Ausgram, has staged a strong comeback, winning by 12,535 votes.The results highlight a significant shift in voter sentiment, with grassroots candidates and emotionally resonant figures playing a key role in shaping the outcome of the West Bengal Assembly elections.

articles.title: West Bengal Assembly term ends May 6: New government likely to take oath on this date
articles.description: According to the Election Commission schedule, if the electoral process concludes by May 6, it marks the end of the current assembly term, paving the way for next government's formation.

They reaffirmed the central government's commitment to the state's development. Actor-turned-politician Vijay's party, TVK, made a significant debut, winning many seats. This marks a potential shift in Tamil Nadu's political landscape.

articles.title: Mamatata: Routed Mamata Banerjee fumes at SIRprise in West Bengal
articles.description: Mamata Banerjee's 15-year reign in Bengal has concluded dramatically with a surprising electoral defeat, shattering her hopes for a historic fourth term and undermining a critical pillar of the INDIA alliance. Her relentless drive and progressive welfare initiatives couldn't withstand the dual challenges of anti-incumbency sentiments and rising corruption claims.

articles.title: South Korean-operated vessel ablaze in Strait of Hormuz; Trump says Iran fired at ship
articles.description: A South Korean ship experienced an explosion and fire in the Strait of Hormuz. U.S. President Trump stated Iran was responsible for the attack. No casualties were reported. Authorities are investigating the cause of the blaze. The incident occurred as the U.S. initiated an operation to secure shipping lanes. South Korea is reviewing intelligence and considering its response.

High-profile defeats included senior ministers and the deputy mayor, marking a stark contrast to previous elections. While some victories offered solace, the overall results signal a major shift in the city's political landscape.

articles.description: Actor Vijay's TVK won 107 seats in a stunning political debut in Tamil Nadu, falling 11 short of a majority. The party, which defeated CM MK Stalin in his own constituency, is now seeking support from smaller parties to form a government.

articles.title: Mandaviya To Oversee Election Of BJP’s Puducherry Legislature Party Leader
articles.description:
New Delhi, May 4 (PTI) The BJP on Monday appointed Union minister Mansukh Mandaviya as the central observer for election of its legislative party leader in Puducherry.

This came after the AINRC-led NDA emerged victorious in the April 9 Puducherry Assembly elections and it was all set to form government for yet another term.

"Parliamentary board of the Bharatiya Janata Party has appointed Dr Mansukh Mandaviya, Union Minister, and Nirmala Kumar Surana, BJP in-charge Puducherry, as central observers for the election of the leader of the legislative party in Puducherry," BJP national general secretary Arun Singh said in a notification.

AINRC leader and Chief Minister N Rangasamy won from both the seats that he contested -- Thattanchavady and Mangalam -- even as the party won 12 seats, according to the latest Election Commission figures.

Elections for 30 seats were held on April 9. The territorial assembly also has three nominated members to the House.

NDA constituent BJP won from four seats while other coalition members -- AIADMK and LJK -- won from one seat each.

(This report has been published as part of the auto-generated syndicate wire feed. Apart from the headline, no editing has been done in the copy by ABP Live.)

articles.title: Was BJP’s Bet On RG Kar Victim’s Mother Ratna Debnath The Game-Changer In Panihati?
articles.description:
Ratna Debnath, the mother of the young woman who was raped and killed in Kolkata's RG Kar Medical College and Hospital, won Panihati seat from the Bharatiya Janata Party ticket in the West Bengal assembly election, Sher defeated Trinamool Congress's Tirthankar Ghosh by 28,836 votes.

Ratna Debnath entered the electoral fray with a strong campaign against the Mamata Banerjee government, focusing on rising crimes against women in the state. She positioned the issue at the centre of her outreach to voters.

In recent years, West Bengal has witnessed several incidents that raised serious concerns about governance and institutional accountability. The murder of a young doctor at RG Kar Medical College and Hospital triggered widespread outrage across the state.

Although protests initially gained momentum, they gradually subsided, with some participants alleging pressure and punitive actions, including transfers. Several of these actions were later challenged in the Calcutta High Court.

TMC Loses In Panihati After 2011

Nirmal Ghosh, TMC's previous contendor, had emerged victorious since 2011 in Panihati, proving to be the stronghold for the party. He won the seat for Trinamool Congress in 2011. He defeated CPI(M)’s Ahibhushan Bhattacharya by 31,432 votes in 2011, and managed to retain the seat in 2016, defeating Sanmoy Bandopadhyay of the Congress party by a reduced 3,030 votes. He defeated Sanmoy Bandopadhyay again in 2021, who contested as a nominee of the BJP, by an increased margin of 25,177 votes.

Was It A Right Move By BJP?

Prime Minister Narendra Modi said that standing with and nominating the mother of the RG Kar rape-murder victim as a party candidate in the West Bengal Assembly elections has proved the Bharatiya Janata Party’s commitment towards women’s safety in the state. Debnath, who reportedly has not combed her hair since the incident as a form of mourning and protest, had said that she will continue this until justice is served for her daughter.

A Mother who was waiting for the justice of his daughter might finally get a fair chance to give punishment to the actual accused with her victory and it proved to be a right move by BJP. It can be helpful for the mother Debnath to continue her fight against the system or even can make it better.

articles.title: Alangulam election results 2026: DMK’s Paul Manoj Pandian wins by 7,798 votes
articles.description: DMK's Paul Manoj Pandian secured victory in the Alangulam Assembly election by a margin of 7,798 votes. The constituency, located in Tenkasi district, faces persistent issues including irrigation shortages, poor infrastructure, and limited healthcare and education facilities. Youth unemployment remains a significant concern for the underdeveloped region.

articles.title: Bahour election result 2026: DMK's R Senthil Kumar emerges as the winner with 10520 votes
articles.description: DMK's R Senthil Kumar has secured victory in the Bahour constituency, defeating T Thiagarajan by 2397 votes in the 2026 assembly elections. This agrarian region in southern Puducherry witnessed a remarkable 93.99% voter turnout by 7 pm. Bahour's electoral landscape has seen shifting loyalties, with Kumar's win marking a continuation of this trend.

articles.title: ‘Lotus Blooms In Bengal’: PM Modi, Amit Shah’s First Reaction After BJP’s Landslide Win
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi welcomed BJP’s massive lead in the West Bengal Assembly Elections, calling it a historic mandate and a victory of good governance. Home Minister Amit Shah said the results reflect people’s rejection of fear and appeasement politics. With BJP leading in over 195 seats, celebrations spread across Kolkata, beginning with jhalmuri gatherings and turning into a full Bengali feast at the Salt Lake headquarters. Party workers served fish curry, rice, and sweets to hundreds. The festive mood continues as counting remains underway, with the TMC trailing and final results yet to be officially declared.

articles.title: PM Modi wears traditional Bengali dhoti and kurta as BJP clean sweeps West Bengal for first time
articles.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi donned a traditional Bengali dhoti at BJP headquarters following the party's decisive victory in West Bengal. The BJP is set to form its first government, ending the TMC's 15-year rule with a significant majority. This win signifies a major political shift, with the BJP's vote share increasing substantially.

articles.title: Sivakasi election results 2026: Keerthana S of TVK wins, Congress’ Ashokan G finishes second
articles.description: Keerthana S of Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam won the election. Ashokan G of Indian National Congress came second. Sivakasi saw a political shift in 2021. For 2026, Ashokan, Rajenthrabhalaji, Diana, and Keerthana are candidates. Ashokan won in 2021. Rajenthrabhalaji won in 2016 and 2011. AIADMK historically dominated the constituency.

articles.title: BENGAL’S POLITICAL EARTHQUAKE: BJP NEARS 200 SEATS TRINAMOOL TRAILS AS STATE DEMANDS CHANGE
articles.description: West Bengal is rewriting its political map. BJP is closing in on 200 seats in a 293-seat assembly, crossing the majority mark, while Trinamool Congress — which has ruled the state for over a decade — trails significantly. A record 92% voter turnout, returning migrant workers, first-time voters, and years of built-up anti-incumbency have all converged in one historic verdict. Mamata Banerjee holds her personal seat in Bhabanipur, but her party’s ground machinery has cracked across the state. North Bengal, Junglemahal, and even traditional TMC strongholds are shifting. This isn’t a sudden surge — it’s a slow tide that finally broke. West Bengal has seen political earthquakes before. This may be another one.

articles.description: Kerala CM: Congress weighs Satheesan, Chennithala and Venugopal after UDF victory, as coalition pressures, leadership claims and bypoll concerns shape decision making, with allies seeking cabinet share and party balancing power in the state
